title: Binary Search
explanation: Binary Search works on sorted answers or sorted arrays by repeatedly checking the middle position.
practiceTip: Write the low, high, and mid update rules carefully on paper before coding.

title: Two Pointers
explanation: Two Pointers moves two indexes through data to shrink or expand a window efficiently.
practiceTip: Start with sorted arrays and short examples to understand pointer movement.

title: DFS
explanation: Depth-First Search explores as far as possible before backtracking in a graph or tree.
practiceTip: Practice marking visited nodes first so you do not repeat work.

title: BFS
explanation: Breadth-First Search visits nodes level by level and is useful for shortest paths in unweighted graphs.
practiceTip: Draw the queue state for a small graph to see why BFS expands in layers.

title: Greedy
explanation: Greedy algorithms make the best local choice each step and rely on a proof that the choice stays optimal.
practiceTip: After solving, explain in one sentence why your local choice is safe.

title: Dynamic Programming
explanation: Dynamic Programming breaks a problem into smaller states and stores answers to avoid repeated work.
practiceTip: Define the state meaning clearly before you write any transitions.
